Here is my full bullet list, followed by a role I'm targeting. Return only a JSON array of the bullet IDs I should keep for this JD. Do not rank them.

1. Migrated the demand model from Excel to a Snowflake and Tableau stack, cutting report refresh from overnight to 11 minutes.
2. Cut manual rate-loading time from 6 hours per week to 40 minutes by scripting the extranet upload in Python.
3. Represents commercial on the loyalty replatform, scoping how member rates flow into the pricing engine.
4. Owns pricing for 41 leisure properties across three US mountain regions, $214M annual room revenue.
5. Piloted dynamic group pricing at four resort properties, converting 22 additional group blocks in the first season.
6. Runs the weekly commercial forecast call with 12 general managers; forecast accuracy holds within 3.1% at 30 days out.
7. Reduced overbooking write-offs 34% by tightening the no-show model with two years of arrival data.
8. Rebuilt the length-of-stay pricing rules for ski-season inventory, lifting RevPAR 9.4% year over year.
9. Trained 9 property-level revenue analysts on the new rate-shopping workflow; all 9 passed internal certification.
10. Caught a channel-parity break across two OTA partners that had been leaking an estimated $380K annually.

Sablewood Resorts Group is hiring a Director of Revenue Strategy to rebuild pricing across 63 owned and managed leisure properties. They want 8+ years in hotel or resort revenue management including at least two years covering a multi-property portfolio, someone who has owned a seasonal pricing strategy end to end (length-of-stay controls, group displacement), comfort writing your own SQL against a Snowflake warehouse, a track record of forecast accuracy inside 5% at 30 days, and working knowledge of OTA channel parity enforcement. The role also touches a loyalty replatform landing next year.

Here is my full bullet list, followed by a role I'm targeting. Return only a JSON array of the bullet IDs I should keep for this JD. Do not rank them.

1. Built the productivity dashboard that leadership uses for monthly staffing decisions across 11 clinics.
2. Led the Epic scheduling-template rebuild for oncology infusion, which removed 1,200 annual double-booked slots.
3. Coordinated the COVID-era conversion of a procedure suite into overflow infusion capacity in 9 days.
4. Wrote the sepsis-screening escalation protocol now used across four sites, cutting time-to-antibiotic to 44 minutes.
5. Manages 38 staff across two ambulatory infusion centers serving roughly 1,900 patients a month.
6. Negotiated a supply contract change on infusion consumables saving $126K a year without a formulary change.
7. Held nurse turnover at 11% during a year when the system average was 23%.
8. Passed two unannounced Joint Commission surveys with zero condition-level findings.
9. Shortened average chair-turnover time from 47 minutes to 31 minutes by resequencing pharmacy prep and nurse assignment.
10. Precepted 14 new graduate nurses through the extended orientation pathway; 13 stayed past 18 months.
11. Chairs the site safety committee; closed 87% of reported near-miss events within 14 days.

Here is my full bullet list, followed by a role I'm targeting. Return only a JSON array of the bullet IDs I should keep for this JD. Do not rank them.

1. Migrated 2.1B rows from a single Postgres primary to a sharded layout with 22 minutes of total write downtime.
2. Cut p99 authorization latency from 840ms to 190ms by removing a synchronous fraud call from the hot path.
3. Mentors 4 engineers; two were promoted to senior in the last review cycle.
4. Technical owner of the ledger service that records roughly 6.8M money movements a day for a consumer payments app.
5. Runs the payments on-call rotation of 9 engineers; reduced pages per week from 31 to 7 over two quarters.
6. Wrote the reconciliation engine that matches processor settlement files against internal ledger, now clearing 99.97% of lines automatically.
7. Presented the ledger sharding approach at an internal engineering summit attended by about 300 people.
8. Redesigned double-entry posting to be idempotent, eliminating a class of duplicate-credit incidents that had cost $410K in remediation.
9. Introduced contract testing between the ledger and 6 consuming teams, dropping integration rollbacks from 5 a quarter to 0.
10. Led the PCI-DSS scope reduction that took cardholder data out of 14 services and left 3.
